subject: [PATCH v2] fs: push nr_cached_objects memcg gating into individual filesystems

2026-07-16.patch_summary: This patch moves the check for whether a filesystem's nr_cached_objects hook is memcg-aware from fs/super.c to individual filesystems, allowing each filesystem to handle its own memcg awareness independently. The patch introduces a helper function mem_cgroup_shrink_is_root() in <linux/memcontrol.h> to simplify this check. This change fixes an issue where the assumption that non-root memcgs are not aware of nr_cached_objects was incorrect for XFS, which intentionally drives inode reclaim from per-memcg contexts. The patch also removes a gate in fs/super.c and adds support for XFS's inode-reclaim hook to be driven from per-memcg contexts.

reviews.author: Christian Brauner
reviews.summary: Reviewer requested that the patch author report any outstanding bugs in a new review, allowing the patch to be dropped from linux-next.
reviews.raw_body:
Applied to the vfs.fixes branch of the vfs/vfs.git tree.
Patches in the vfs.fixes branch should appear in linux-next soon.

Please report any outstanding bugs that were missed during review in a
new review to the original patch series allowing us to drop it.

It's encouraged to provide Acked-bys and Reviewed-bys even though the
patch has now been applied. If possible patch trailers will be updated.

Note that commit hashes shown below are subject to change due to rebase,
trailer updates or similar. If in doubt, please check the listed branch.

tree:   https://git.kernel.org/pub/scm/linux/kernel/git/vfs/vfs.git
branch: vfs.fixes

[1/1] fs: push nr_cached_objects memcg gating into individual filesystems
      https://git.kernel.org/vfs/vfs/c/d5233434ecd5
